questions.q: What is Exception Handling in Java?
questions.answer: Exception Handling in Java is a mechanism to handle runtime errors or exceptions that occur during the execution of a program.
key_points: It prevents the program from crashing.
It provides information about the error.

questions.q: What is the purpose of try block in Exception Handling?
questions.answer: The try block contains the code that may raise an exception. It provides a block of code where we can handle the exception.
key_points: It contains the code that may raise an exception.
It is used to handle the exception.

questions.q: What is the purpose of catch block in Exception Handling?
questions.answer: The catch block contains the code that will be executed if an exception is raised in the try block.
key_points: It contains the code that will be executed if an exception is raised.
It is used to handle the exception.

questions.q: What is finally block in Exception Handling?
questions.answer: The finally block contains the code that will be executed regardless of whether an exception is raised or not.
key_points: It contains the code that will be executed regardless of whether an exception is raised or not.
It is used to release system resources.

questions.q: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions in Java?
questions.answer: Checked exceptions are those that can be caught and handled at compile time, while unchecked exceptions are those that cannot be caught and handled at compile time.
key_points: Checked exceptions are those that can be caught and handled at compile time.
Unchecked exceptions are those that cannot be caught and handled at compile time.

questions.q: What are the types of exceptions in Java?
questions.answer: The types of exceptions in Java are runtime exceptions and checked exceptions.
key_points: Runtime exceptions are those that occur at runtime.
Checked exceptions are those that can be caught and handled at compile time.

questions.q: What is the purpose of throws keyword in Java?
questions.answer: The throws keyword is used to declare that a method may throw a particular type of exception.
key_points: It is used to declare that a method may throw a particular type of exception.
It is used to declare that a method may throw multiple types of exceptions.

questions.q: How to handle multiple exceptions in Java?
questions.answer: To handle multiple exceptions in Java, we can use multiple catch blocks or a single catch block with a parameterized type.
key_points: We can use multiple catch blocks to handle multiple exceptions.
We can use a single catch block with a parameterized type to handle multiple exceptions.

questions.q: What is the order of execution of try, catch, and finally blocks in Java?
questions.answer: The order of execution of try, catch, and finally blocks in Java is try, catch, and finally.
key_points: Try block is executed first.
Catch block is executed if an exception is raised.
Finally block is executed regardless of whether an exception is raised or not.

questions.q: How to rethrow an exception in Java?
questions.answer: To rethrow an exception in Java, we can use the throw keyword inside the catch block.
key_points: We can use the throw keyword to rethrow an exception.
We can use a rethrow method to rethrow an exception.

questions.q: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions in terms of compilation?
questions.answer: Checked exceptions must be caught and handled at compile time, while unchecked exceptions do not need to be caught and handled at compile time.
key_points: Checked exceptions must be caught and handled at compile time.
Unchecked exceptions do not need to be caught and handled at compile time.

questions.q: How to handle a custom exception in Java?
questions.answer: To handle a custom exception in Java, we can create a custom exception class that extends the Exception class and throw it in the catch block.
key_points: We can create a custom exception class that extends the Exception class.
We can throw the custom exception in the catch block.

questions.q: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions in terms of runtime behavior?
questions.answer: Checked exceptions are caught and handled at runtime, while unchecked exceptions are not caught and handled at runtime.
key_points: Checked exceptions are caught and handled at runtime.
Unchecked exceptions are not caught and handled at runtime.

questions.q: How to handle multiple exceptions with different types of exceptions in Java?
questions.answer: To handle multiple exceptions with different types of exceptions in Java, we can use multiple catch blocks with different types of exceptions.
key_points: We can use multiple catch blocks with different types of exceptions.
We can use a single catch block with a parameterized type to handle multiple exceptions.

questions.q: What is the purpose of try-with-resources statement in Java?
questions.answer: The try-with-resources statement is used to automatically close resources such as streams, sockets, and files.
key_points: It is used to automatically close resources.
It is used to prevent resource leaks.

questions.q: How to handle a runtime exception in Java?
questions.answer: To handle a runtime exception in Java, we can use a try-catch block and catch the runtime exception in the catch block.
key_points: We can use a try-catch block to handle a runtime exception.
We can catch the runtime exception in the catch block.
